collector/cmd.purpose: Thin launcher for the benthos-collector binary: a separate Go module whose main.go blank-imports the custom OpenMeter bloblang/input/output plugin packages and then calls service.RunCLI with leader-election CLI options. Contains no pipeline logic of its own.

patterns.name: Blank-import plugins before RunCLI
patterns.description: Custom plugin packages and Benthos/Connect component bundles are registered solely via blank imports (_) so their init() side-effects run before service.RunCLI is invoked. main() must not call plugin constructors directly.

patterns.name: Cancellable root context wired into RunCLI
patterns.description: main() builds a cancellable context.Background() and threads it into both service.RunCLI and leaderelection.GetLeaderElectionCLIOpts so shutdown propagates.
patterns.example: ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background()); defer cancel(); service.RunCLI(ctx, leaderelection.GetLeaderElectionCLIOpts(ctx)...)

patterns.name: version provisioned by ldflags with init() fallback
patterns.description: version is a package var set by ldflags at build time; init() defaults it to 'unknown' when absent.
patterns.example: var version string; func init() { if version == "" { version = "unknown" } }

key_file_guides.role: Entrypoint that blank-imports plugin packages and Benthos component bundles, then calls service.RunCLI with leader-election options.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New custom plugins (bloblang/input/output) must be added as blank imports here or they will not register. Do not add pipeline/business logic — it belongs in collector/benthos.

key_file_guides.role: Holds the ldflags-provisioned version string and the init() fallback to 'unknown'.

anti_patterns: Adding pipeline/business logic to main.go instead of collector/benthos plugin packages
Registering a new plugin by calling its constructor rather than blank-importing the package
Replacing the cancellable context with context.Background() passed directly to RunCLI without cancel propagation

decisions.decision: Built as a separate Go module and Docker image (benthos-collector.Dockerfile, CGO_ENABLED=0).
decisions.rationale: The collector is an independently deployable Redpanda Benthos/Connect service with a distinct dependency tree, kept out of the main module to avoid pulling Benthos into the core binaries.

e2e.purpose: End-to-end test suite that runs against a live OpenMeter stack bootstrapped via Docker Compose. Tests call the real HTTP API through the generated Go client (v1) and a hand-rolled v3Client (v3), asserting full-stack behavior including async Kafka->ClickHouse pipelines. No internal domain packages are imported — only HTTP clients and public type definitions.

patterns.name: OPENMETER_ADDRESS env-gate
patterns.description: Every test file obtains its client via initClient(t) or newV3Client(t), both of which call t.Skip when OPENMETER_ADDRESS is unset. New tests must never bypass this guard.
patterns.example: address := os.Getenv("OPENMETER_ADDRESS"); if address == "" { t.Skip("OPENMETER_ADDRESS not set") }

patterns.name: assert.EventuallyWithT for async pipeline assertions
patterns.description: Any assertion on ClickHouse-derived data (meter queries, entitlement balances) uses assert.EventuallyWithT with a 1-minute timeout and 1-second interval, because Kafka->sink-worker->ClickHouse is async. Never assert synchronously after ingest.
patterns.example: assert.EventuallyWithT(t, func(t *assert.CollectT) { resp, _ := client.QueryMeterWithResponse(...) }, time.Minute, time.Second)

patterns.name: v3Client (status, body, problem) triple
patterns.description: v3 API tests use *v3Client from v3helpers_test.go (not the generated SDK); methods return (statusCode, *T, *v3Problem). Assert statusCode first, then the typed body.
patterns.example: status, plan, problem := c.CreatePlan(body); require.Equal(t, http.StatusCreated, status, "problem: %+v", problem)

patterns.name: Unique keys via timestamp/ULID to avoid shared-DB collisions
patterns.description: Feature/plan/addon/customer keys carry a timestamp or ULID suffix (uniqueKey() helper). Never use hardcoded keys for entities that cannot be idempotently re-created in the shared persistent DB.

patterns.name: shouldRunSlowTests guard for minute-resolution assertions
patterns.description: Tests that sleep >5s (waiting for a new minute for entitlement reset or credit granularity) must be gated behind shouldRunSlowTests(t), which checks the RUN_SLOW_TESTS env var.

patterns.name: Dedicated v3 error-path assertion helpers
patterns.description: Negative-path tests use assertValidationCode (extensions.validationErrors[].code), assertInvalidParameterRule (schema invalid_parameters[].rule), and assertProblemDetail (substring in Detail) from v3helpers_test.go.

patterns.name: Meter definitions in config.yaml before test ingest
patterns.description: Every event type ingested by tests must have a corresponding meter slug in e2e/config.yaml; the sink-worker validates events against known meters and silently drops events for unknown meters.
patterns.example:
meters:
  - slug: plan_meter
    eventType: plan_meter
    aggregation: SUM

key_file_guides.role: Provides initClient (v1 SDK client) and shouldRunSlowTests — the single source of truth for v1 bootstrapping and env-var guards.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding setup logic that skips rather than fails will silently ignore tests in CI when the env var is missing.

key_file_guides.role: Defines *v3Client (raw HTTP), v3Problem (RFC 7807 parse), decodeTyped generic, all typed CRUD wrappers, validXxx fixture builders, uniqueKey(), and the assertion helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: v3Client.do uses t.Context() with v3RequestTimeout — do not bypass with context.Background(). Use uniqueKey() for all entity keys.

key_file_guides.role: Shared cross-test helpers: CreateCustomerWithSubject, GetMeterIDBySlug, QueryMeterV3 (raw HTTP v3 meter query).
key_file_guides.watch_for: QueryMeterV3 uses http.DefaultClient and reads OPENMETER_ADDRESS directly; it skips if the address is empty. Do not inline these helpers in test files.

key_file_guides.role: OpenMeter config injected into the Compose stack: meter definitions, dedupe (redis), credits.enabled=false.
key_file_guides.watch_for: credits.enabled=false is a deliberate test contract (TestSettlementMode); do not change without updating tests. Adding a meter slug in a test without a definition here silently drops events.

key_file_guides.role: test-local: tear down, bring up the compose stack, wait for sink-worker /healthz on port 30000, then run go test with TZ=UTC OPENMETER_ADDRESS=http://localhost:38888.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TZ=UTC is essential for deterministic time-window assertions. Port 38888 is the openmeter API; 30000 is the sink-worker healthz.

key_file_guides.role: Overrides image: with build: .. so local tests run the locally built binary.
key_file_guides.watch_for: docker-compose.openmeter-latest.yaml uses the published GHCR image for CI — do not merge the two approaches.

key_file_guides.role: Tests the openmeter-jobs ledger backfill-accounts command via docker compose exec and verifies ledger_customer_accounts rows via direct pgxpool queries.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ensureLocalComposeBackfillSupport skips when OPENMETER_ADDRESS is not the localhost:38888 compose stack — this test cannot run against remote environments.

anti_patterns: Asserting meter query results synchronously after ingest without assert.EventuallyWithT — data flows async through Kafka and ClickHouse
Using hardcoded feature/plan/customer keys shared across test functions — causes 409 or silent state pollution in the shared DB
Importing app/common, Wire provider packages, or Ent adapters — e2e tests must use only HTTP clients and public types
Adding new meters to tests without a corresponding entry in e2e/config.yaml — the sink-worker silently drops events to unknown meters
Using ports in the 30000 range for new services without checking they don't conflict with the root docker-compose.yaml or quickstart (40000 range)

decisions.decision: Two client layers: generated api.ClientWithResponses for v1 and a hand-rolled v3Client for v3.
decisions.rationale: The v3 API was still evolving; a thin wrapper returning (status, *T, *v3Problem) gives tests direct HTTP-level control without fighting the generated SDK's union-type machinery.
TestSettlementMode depends on credits being off to assert that credit_only settlement mode is rejected — a deliberate, documented contract.

decisions.decision: Separate docker-compose.openmeter-local.yaml overriding image: with build:.
decisions.rationale: Makes 'make test-local' always test the locally built binary while CI swaps in a published image via docker-compose.openmeter-latest.yaml without changing test code.

code_examples.scenario: A v3 resource lifecycle test (create -> assert status -> reuse ID)

quickstart.purpose: Self-contained demo environment that boots all OpenMeter services (openmeter, sink-worker, balance-worker, notification-service, billing-worker, openmeter-jobs) via Docker Compose and validates end-to-end ingest->query behavior with quickstart_test.go. Uses port range 40000-49999 to avoid conflicts with the root dev environment and the e2e suite (30000 range).

patterns.name: OPENMETER_ADDRESS env-gate on tests
patterns.description: Both initClient and openmeterAddress check OPENMETER_ADDRESS and call t.Skip if unset; tests never run against an embedded server, only a live Compose stack.
patterns.example: address := os.Getenv("OPENMETER_ADDRESS"); if address == "" { t.Skip("OPENMETER_ADDRESS not set") }

patterns.name: assert.EventuallyWithT for async ingest assertions
patterns.description: All ingest-then-query assertions use EventuallyWithT with a 30-second timeout and 1-second tick to tolerate Kafka/ClickHouse propagation latency.
patterns.example: assert.EventuallyWithT(t, func(t *assert.CollectT) { resp := queryMeterV3(...); require.Len(t, resp.Data, 2) }, 30*time.Second, time.Second)

patterns.name: Port range 40000-49999
patterns.description: All Compose service ports use the 40000 range to avoid collisions with the root dev docker-compose.yaml and the e2e suite (30000 range): openmeter=48888, sink-worker=40000, balance-worker=40001, notification-service=40002, billing-worker=40003.

patterns.name: Separate docker-compose.debug-ports.yaml overlay
patterns.description: docker-compose.debug-ports.yaml adds host port bindings for Kafka/ClickHouse/Redis/Postgres/Svix only when debugging; the base docker-compose.yaml exposes only application-level ports. make test-local merges both files.

patterns.name: Unique meter slugs per test run via timestamp suffix
patterns.description: quickstart_test.go generates v1MeterSlug and v3MeterKey with a UnixNano suffix to avoid collisions on shared persistent DB state across runs.

patterns.name: autoMigrate: migration in config.yaml
patterns.description: Uses the Atlas SQL migration path (autoMigrate: migration), not the development-only ent schema create, exercising the real production migration pipeline.

key_file_guides.role: End-to-end smoke test: creates meters via v1 and v3 APIs, ingests CloudEvents with fixed IDs (00001/00002/00003), and asserts meter query values for both API versions using EventuallyWithT.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses context.Background() in helpers — acceptable for an integration test, not application code. Event IDs and expected count values (2 + 1 window split) are intentionally fixed; new tests must use unique suffixes.

key_file_guides.role: Full-stack compose: all five OpenMeter binaries plus infra (Kafka, ClickHouse, Redis, Postgres, Svix), referencing docker-compose.base.yaml via extends.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New binaries must be added here with matching command, config volume mount, and depends_on health checks. openmeter-jobs runs 'quickstart cron' (not a long-running server) — do not give it a healthcheck.

key_file_guides.role: Minimal production-like config: sample meters, Svix configured, portal enabled, autoMigrate: migration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New required app/config fields must be reflected here or the stack silently fails to start. The Svix apiKey is a test JWT, not sensitive.

key_file_guides.role: test-local: spin up stack (both compose files), health-check sink-worker port 40000, run tests with OPENMETER_ADDRESS=http://localhost:48888, tear down.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The health check hits sink-worker port 40000 (not the openmeter API 48888). TZ is not set here (unlike e2e) — tests use fixed UTC timestamps in event data instead.

key_file_guides.role: Operator-facing quickstart walkthrough: launch via docker compose up, ingest CloudEvents via curl, query meters, configure additional meters.
key_file_guides.watch_for: curl examples use port 48888 and event IDs 00001/00002/00003 matching the test; keep them in sync with config.yaml meter slugs and quickstart_test.go.

anti_patterns: Using port numbers outside the 40000 range — they conflict with the root docker-compose.yaml or the e2e suite
Running quickstart_test.go without a live Compose stack — tests must guard with t.Skip on missing OPENMETER_ADDRESS
Hardcoding event IDs or meter slugs without a unique suffix (the fixed event IDs are intentional only for the canonical test)
Omitting depends_on health checks when adding a new service to docker-compose.yaml
Setting autoMigrate to 'ent' instead of 'migration' — this bypasses the Atlas migration pipeline and hides regressions

decisions.decision: quickstart_test.go uses the generated Go SDK (api/client/go) for v1 but raw HTTP helpers for v3.
decisions.rationale: Demonstrates intended SDK usage for v1 while covering the v3 surface, which lacks a generated client helper matching the test's assertion patterns.

decisions.decision: All five OpenMeter binaries plus openmeter-jobs are declared in docker-compose.yaml.
decisions.rationale: Quickstart is the canonical smoke-test for new operators and must demonstrate the full production topology, not a reduced subset.

decisions.decision: Port range 40000-49999 reserved exclusively for quickstart.
decisions.rationale: Avoids conflicts with both the root dev environment and the e2e suite (30000 range), letting all three run simultaneously on one machine.

code_examples.scenario: Assert meter query value after async ingest using EventuallyWithT
